generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance TemperatureBedActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "A regulated heating element ensuring uniform temperature distribution across the incubation chamber"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Operates at 0.02 m³/s airflow with a bang-bang controller (12V or 24V power supply)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring internal temperature (-55°C to +125°C, ±0.5°C accuracy) via 1-Wire protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring internal temperature (-55°C to +125°C, ±0.5°C accuracy) via 1-Wire protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring external temperature (-55°C to +125°C, ±0.5°C accuracy) via 1-Wire protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data stream from all internal/external sensors (raw, ±0.5°C accuracy)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

instance ProcessedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and normalized temperature data after preprocessing for model validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingModule
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalIncubatorGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric CAD representation of the insulated thermal incubator enclosure"
]

instance ThermalBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model capturing thermal dynamics and heat distribution within the incubation chamber"
]

instance ControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control theory framework for optimizing fan and heatbed actuator performance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms
]

instance CalibrationParameterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model for updating parameter estimates using calibration algorithms from real-time sensor data"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQDataPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message queuing server facilitating real-time data distribution between Physical Twin and Digital Twin via periodic temperature readings"
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithms : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Set of algorithms updating model parameters with best estimates derived from sensor inputs for model validation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationParameterModel
]

instance DataPreprocessingModule : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Module performing filtering and normalization of raw sensor data before integration into DT's data pipeline"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance FaultDetectionSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms identifying sensor or system failures via state estimation and deviation analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance StateEstimationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Inference system estimating hidden states (e.g., internal temperature gradients) from noisy sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance OptimizerController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic framework minimizing energy use or maximizing incubation efficiency via data-model interactions"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data collection and validation of Physical Twin performance via sensor inputs"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureMonitoringInsight, OperationalStatusInsight
]

instance ValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model validation through iterative calibration against real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithmic-driven improvements to system performance and efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyOptimizationAction, ParameterTuningAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based forecasting of Physical Twin behavior under current/anticipated conditions"
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous or human-guided adjustments to maintain optimal/system-safe operation"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionAction, SafetyConstraintAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard integration for intuitive communication among engineers"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from all sensors with spatial distribution and accuracy (±0.5°C)"
]

instance OperationalStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive status of incubation conditions including temperature uniformity, humidity (implied), and CO₂ levels"
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confidence measure of model calibration accuracy derived from sensor data alignment"
]

instance EfficiencyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated parameter adjustments to maximize incubation efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual or automated adjustment of heatbed/fan actuator parameters for optimal performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance FaultDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered when sensor data indicates potential faults requiring immediate intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyConstraintAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated corrective actions to maintain safe operating conditions (e.g., temperature thresholds)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DashboardVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive graphical representation of incubation parameters for real-time monitoring"
]

// C1: Environment
instance ThermalIncubationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureBedActuator, FanActuator,
                 InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor,
                 RabbitMQDataPipeline, CalibrationAlgorithms, DataPreprocessingModule,
                 TemperatureDataStream, ProcessedTemperatureData
]

instance BiomedicalApplicationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ThermalIncubatorGeometryModel, ThermalBehaviorModel,
                 ControlSystemModel, CalibrationParameterModel,
                 MonitoringService, ValidationService, OptimizationService,
                 PredictionService, ControlService, VisualizationService,
                 OperationalOptimizationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** involves control commands sent from the DT to the PT, specifically for controlling a **fan** and **heatbed**. These commands are transmitted through the **RabbitMQ communication server** running on the Raspberry Pi, which acts as the intermediary for control signals. While the exact message formats and authentication mechanisms are not detailed in the documents, the existence of such control flows is confirmed. Feedback systems are implied for monitoring command execution and system response, enabling adaptive control strategies (e.g., supervisory controllers) to ensure optimal and safe operation of the PT.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system in the provided context emphasizes real-time synchronization between the Physical Twin (PT) and its Digital Twin through continuous data collection via sensors. While exact synchronization frequencies are not specified, the DT relies on **calibration algorithms** to dynamically update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with the latest parameter estimates. This ensures temporal alignment between the digital and physical representations, enabling accurate monitoring and predictive analysis under current operating conditions. The temporal granularity is implied to support **real-time decision-making** (e.g., fault detection, state estimation) where violations of safety or optimality trigger immediate interventions. Latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, but the integration of algorithms for **state estimation and supervisory control** suggests a need for sub-second responsiveness to maintain coherence between the DT and PT."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT system is described as a **framework integrating modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation technologies** with a centralized structure. The core components include:
  1. **Data**: Collected continuously from the PT via sensors over time, forming the foundational input for the DT.
  2. **Models**: Abstract representations of both cyber and physical aspects of the PT and its environment, updated via **calibration algorithms** to reflect real-world conditions.
  3. **Algorithms**: Techniques such as fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimisers, which process data and models to generate insights or actions.
  The orchestration involves a **closed-loop system** where calibration updates models, monitors system performance, and triggers interventions (human or algorithmic) upon detected violations. The architecture pattern is **monolithic** (centralized), with no explicit mention of microservices, cloud-native patterns, or distributed coordination mechanisms. Resource management is implied to be handled internally by the DT’s integrated algorithms and models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle encompasses phases that integrate **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation** technologies while maintaining alignment with the Physical Twin (PT). Key stages include:
    - **Design Phase**: Creation of abstract models (e.g., CAD models) focusing on static attributes (e.g., wheel sizes, chassis shapes) without dynamic behaviour. Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations) are later incorporated for analysis.
    - **Simulation & Validation Phase**: Development of dynamic models to predict PT behaviour under operating conditions. Models are calibrated using sensor data from the PT to refine parameters (e.g., via **calibration algorithms**).
    - **Operational Phase**: Continuous integration of **data** (collected via sensors), **models** (cyber-physical and environmental), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) for real-time monitoring and optimisation. Violations trigger interventions via human or automated decision-making.
    - **Evolution Phase**: Models and algorithms are updated iteratively to adapt to changes in the PT or environment, ensuring long-term relevance. The lifecycle emphasises **lifelong integration** of models into the DT framework rather than discarding them post-deployment.

    **Transition Management**: The shift from static to dynamic models and the integration of sensor data require **calibration algorithms** to align DT models with PT performance. Governance involves ensuring consistency between cyber and physical systems, with emphasis on **safety and optimality checks** during operational phases.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
    **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    The DT’s models must achieve **high fidelity** to accurately reproduce the PT’s behavior, particularly in dynamic scenarios (e.g., force-driven movements in [3, Section 5]). This requires:
    - **Parameter Accuracy:** Models must be calibrated using real-time sensor data to minimize discrepancies between digital and physical twins.
    - **Uncertainty Quantification:** Sources of uncertainty (e.g., sensor noise, environmental variability) must be explicitly modeled to assess prediction confidence.

    **Validation and Verification Methods:**
    - **Dynamic Validation:** Models are validated through **simulation-to-reality comparisons**, where predicted behaviors (e.g., car dynamics) are cross-checked against PT performance under identical conditions.
    - **Error Detection:** Calibration algorithms identify deviations (e.g., via residuals in model-data fits) and trigger corrections, ensuring continuous alignment.
    - **Quality Assurance:** A **closed-loop validation process** (data → model → feedback) is enforced to maintain fidelity over time.

    **Uncertainty and Safety Mechanisms:**
    - **Error Correction:** Algorithms (e.g., state estimators) dynamically adjust models based on sensor feedback, reducing cumulative errors.
    - **Safety Gates:** Monitors detect violations (e.g., suboptimal performance) and trigger interventions (human or automated) to restore PT safety/operational integrity.
    - **Fidelity Metrics:** While not explicitly quantified in the documents, the DT’s structure implies iterative validation loops to sustain high-fidelity operation.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** is implemented using a **RabbitMQ server running on a Raspberry Pi**, serving as the central communication hub. The network topology is **on-premises**, with no mention of cloud or distributed architectures. Communication protocols are **RabbitMQ’s message-based system**, though specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, TCP/IP) are not detailed. The system does not specify bandwidth or latency requirements, but the deployment on a Raspberry Pi implies minimal performance demands. Reliability is ensured by the RabbitMQ broker’s redundancy mechanisms (e.g., message persistence and acknowledgment-based delivery), though no explicit security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication) are mentioned for network communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment** is deployed **on-premises** on a **Raspberry Pi**, with the RabbitMQ server running locally. The computational requirements are not specified beyond the Raspberry Pi’s capabilities, and storage needs are similarly unspecified. The deployment model is **static**, as no mention of containerization (e.g., Docker), orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes), or dynamic scaling is provided. Availability and reliability are not explicitly guaranteed; the system’s performance is tied to the Raspberry Pi’s hardware constraints, with no mention of redundancy or failover mechanisms beyond RabbitMQ’s built-in features."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) framework integrates calibration algorithms to update cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental models with real-time sensor data for parameter estimation. A fail-safe mechanism is explicitly mentioned, where a 'safe protocol is incorporated into the low-level driver' to ensure the device remains in a controlled operational state upon failure. This implies inherent safety-critical safeguards, but no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols, access control, authentication, threat mitigation, or risk assessment frameworks is provided in the documents."
]
